prompt: At standard atmospheric pressure (1 atm, 101.325 kPa), state (a) the boiling point of water in degrees Celsius and (b) the freezing point of water in degrees Celsius. Give both numbers.
notes: Correct facts at 1 atm: boiling 100 C, freezing 0 C. keywords_all requires BOTH numbers (the digit-boundary guard means the required '0' will not be satisfied by the '0' inside '100'). contains_none plants the common wrong claim 'water boils at 90 C' as a must-not-appear contradiction, so a plausible-but-wrong answer fails. Balanced: a right-and-clean answer passes; the planted contradiction fails.
